_readme: duel/data/duel-stats.json — THE single source of truth for every duel move.
Edit here, then `node duel/gen-stats.mjs` (emits duel/src/stats_gen.h AND
tf-frontend/src/data/duel-stats.ts), then `bash duel/build.sh test|golden|parity`.
=== THE LOCKED PRINCIPLE: RARITY BUYS VERBS, NOT NUMBERS ===
Duels STAKE A REAL TREAT and are bracketed by SWEETNESS ONLY — so a q1 Common and a q4
Epic at the same sweetness WILL be matched, with treats on the line. If the Epic could
win on numbers alone the game would be pay-to-win. It cannot. A fighter only ever rolls
moves of its OWN quality (database/recipe.cpp), so a quality's move pool IS its toolkit:
q1  damage, block, shield                         — the honest, FAST, always-ready baseline
q2  damage, shield, AoE                           — the first splash
q3  damage, shield, counter, heal, life-siphon, double-strike — sustain and punish
q4  damage, block, guard, counter, GROUP-SHIELD, four AoEs, group-heal, two ultimates
THE TWO AXES, and they are not the same axis:
SWEETNESS is the STAT axis   — max_hp = hp_base + sweetness * hp_per_sweetness. It is also
the matchmaking bracket, so it is fair BY CONSTRUCTION.
QUALITY   is the TOOLKIT axis — it buys VERBS. It buys ZERO stats. `hp_per_quality` is 0,
and it must STAY 0: two treats in one sweetness bracket have
IDENTICAL max HP regardless of quality. That is the intent,
literally: "you should be able to fight anyone, and is scaled
so they are the same, but better treats have better attacks
and abilities."
=== WHAT `power` MEANS (audited against duel/src/engine.cpp's resolve loop) ===
One column, TEN effects — the rule, effect by effect:
damage       damage dealt by one strike (x `hits` strikes, each clashing separately)
siphon       damage dealt; the caster then heals siphon_pct_256 of what it actually DRAINED
aoe          damage dealt to EVERY living foe, each scaled by aoe_pct_256
block        damage dealt by the swing that comes WITH the stance (a block move DOES hit)
counter      damage dealt by the swing that comes WITH the stance (a counter move DOES hit)
shield       shield points granted to the caster (no damage at all)
heal         health restored to one ally, self included (no damage at all)
groupheal    health restored to EVERY living ally, scaled by aoe_pct_256 (no damage at all)
groupshield  shield points granted to EVERY living ally INCLUDING THE CASTER, scaled by
aoe_pct_256 (no damage at all). Reaching everyone costs per-head potency —
the same tax an AoE and a group-heal pay, for the same reason.
guard        *** UNUSED ***. The guard branch deals no damage and never reads power; it is
pinned at the table minimum (10) so nobody mistakes it for a lever. Give it a
job in the engine before giving it a number.
=== THE TRADE RULE ===
A verb-carrying move TRADES damage for its utility:
- heal / groupheal / guard / shield / groupshield deal NO damage — the turn IS the utility.
- an AoE's RAW power is a normal blow of its tier; aoe_pct_256 then taxes it per victim
(so it never gets its reach for free). Same for a group-heal and a group-shield.
- a siphon hits for slightly less than a plain blow of its tier (it gets the sustain).
- a counter/block hits for much less than a plain blow (it gets the stance).
- a max_uses ultimate may be genuinely powerful — the limited uses are what pay for it.
=== THE STANCE LADDERS ARE FLAT. LITERALLY FLAT. ===
The same verb is the SAME MOVE at every quality — same power, same cooldown, SAME SPEED:
shield   28 / cd1 / spd45   q1 Sugar Shield · q2 Chewy Absorption · q3 Candied Shell
block    12 / cd0 / spd46   q1 Gum Drop Kick · q4 Tarnising Knee Drop
counter  22 / cd1 / spd46   q3 Berry Bounce · q4 Bouncing Barrage
A q1's shield and a q3's shield are INTERCHANGEABLE. That is not redundancy — a fighter
only rolls its own quality's moves, so each quality NEEDS its own copy of the baseline verbs.
This replaced a ladder of 14/cd0 · 28/cd1 · 42/cd2 · 56/cd3, which was defended as "flat,
because power/(cooldown+1) is 14 at every tier". *** THAT WAS AN ARTIFACT OF THE METRIC. ***
A TURN is the scarce resource, not a cooldown. Per turn spent the ladder was 14:28:42:56 — a
clean 4x. Over four rounds a q1 had to shield FOUR times (four turns, ALL of its offence) to
bank 56 points; a q4 spent ONE turn for the same 56 and attacked with the other three.
*** WHEN YOU JUDGE A DEFENSIVE MOVE, DIVIDE BY TURNS SPENT, NEVER BY COOLDOWN. ***
=== HOW A q4 IS STILL SPECIAL: A NEW VERB, NOT A BIGGER NUMBER ===
Heavy Gumdrop Kick kept its 56 power and became a **groupshield**. (56*128)>>8 = 28 — so its
caster banks EXACTLY the 28 a q1's Sugar Shield banks for its turn. The number is gone. What
the Epic buys is REACH: those 28 points land on its WHOLE TEAM. A verb, not a stat. And it
pays for the reach in RATE — at cd3 the caster self-shields 7 points/round against a q1's 14.
=== WHY THE ALL-AoE q4 KIT IS ALLOWED (record the RIGHT reason) ===
q4 can roll four AoEs and field all four: 48.8 SPREAD damage/round against a full team, next
to a q1's 28.0. This was once defended as "a 1-in-715 roll". *** THAT IS A CATEGORY ERROR,
off by ~200x. *** 715 = C(13,4) counts LOADOUT SUBSETS — but the player CHOOSES the loadout.
What is ROLLED is the 9 moves drawn from the 13-move q4 pool, which holds 4 AoEs, and
P(>= 4 AoEs among 9 of 13) ~ 27%. MORE THAN A QUARTER of Epics can field the all-AoE kit.
It is still fine, but for a completely different and much better reason:
*** SPREAD DAMAGE NEVER REMOVES AN ENEMY ACTION. ***
An all-AoE kit kills NOBODY until everyone dies at once, so it eats the enemy's FULL output
for the whole fight. Focused fire cuts enemy throughput by 1/3 with every kill (team_size 3),
and KO is permanent. And that same all-AoE kit's SINGLE-TARGET focus is only 16.3/round —
barely half a q1's 28.0. THAT is what pays for the 48.8. Do not "trust the 1-in-715" to
justify buffing an AoE; there is no such rarity, and there never was.
=== PLAIN DAMAGE: THE THROUGHPUT BUDGET (read this before touching a power) ===
*** A COOLDOWN IS ALMOST FREE AT loadout_size 4. *** A fighter holds FOUR moves, so a move of
cooldown C is castable every round as long as the kit holds C+1 of them. Best-kit damage per
round is therefore NOT power/(cooldown+1) — it is the MEAN OF THE TOP (C+1) BURSTS the pool
can rotate. That mean is the THROUGHPUT BUDGET, and it must be the same (~28) at EVERY quality:
q1  all cd0  -> budget = its single biggest burst          = 28
q2  all cd1  -> budget = mean of its top 2                 = 29
q3  cd1-2    -> budget = mean of its top 3                 ~ 29
q4  cd1-3    -> budget = mean of its top 3-4               ~ 29
What rarity buys inside that fixed budget is SHAPE: the rarer treat's damage is SPIKIER — a
bigger single burst, paid for with weaker filler and a longer wait. Same throughput, more
drama, more decisions. `duel/test/duel_tests --balance-report` MEASURES this by simulating
every 4-move subset; its COMBAT POWER table (best-kit focused dpr x max_hp) is the gate. Keep
=== SPEED (and the claim that used to be here, which was FALSE) ===
This file used to assert "q1's moves are the FASTEST in the game". They are not: q2's Come
Over Here and Slip on it is speed 99 and q1's best is 98. The TRUE statements, which is what
q1's tempo identity actually rests on:
- q1 has the fastest move you can play EVERY ROUND (Coco Chaos, 98, cd0). The one move that
beats it on paper is on a cooldown, so it cannot be held.
- q1 has the highest AVERAGE speed (65.3), and EVERY q1 move is cd0 — it never rotates down
to a weaker option, and it never has a dead turn. That is the real Common advantage.
- Heavy is the SLOW type at every quality (q4's own biggest blow, Silver Knuckles, is speed
30 — its slowest move too). "The hardest hitter is the slowest" is a TYPE rule, not a
handicap the Commons carry alone.

0090580c-04ef-9d84-e883-32f52c977b98.description: The honest stance: a light gumdrop kick, and for the rest of the round you shrug off most of whatever lands on you. It is ready again the very next round — anyone can hold this wall all fight. Every block in the game is exactly this block, whatever the treat cost.

01b34a91-37d8-29d4-09cb-2d515f51e315.description: Binds you to an ALLY in shimmering caramel: for the rest of the round every single-target blow aimed at them lands on YOU instead, and lands softer for the trouble. Pick an ally, not an enemy. It deals no damage — the whole turn is spent covering someone.

1a67cc79-b97a-4624-ca67-36c9fcbee970.description: A flashing mercury-quick slash — almost nothing in the arena moves before it does, and it still lands with weight. Needs a breath to line up again.

1d1dec74-ca35-0cc4-d930-164786a9dd81.description: A frantic chocolate-fisted flurry — the fastest thing in the pantry. Light, but it always goes first, and it is always ready.

1e47a8e0-acfd-1244-0b7d-539d62429060.description: A swift dollop of icing flicked like a whip — blisteringly fast, and never on cooldown.

2569d967-c676-8b44-cbdf-d514e4c5c73d.description: A sticky toffee snare that drinks the foe in as they stumble: a blow that also DRAINS. You heal for a share of the health it actually takes off them — so a hit their shield swallowed whole, or one that finds them already down, feeds you nothing. It hits a little softer than a plain blow; the sustain is what you are paying for.

29d559bb-41a7-9f14-5bf8-605da8166943.description: A sour sucker clamped on and yanked away — plain, dependable, and ready every single round.

2c555752-8a84-58f4-395e-6460b7864069.description: Yanks the fight to close range with a taffy-rope pull before landing the hit. Simple, and always available.

2c94f949-a10e-2524-2827-6588982ce0b1.description: A springy berry-skin stance: strike, then bounce a good part of any blow that lands on you this round straight back at whoever threw it. It soaks NOTHING — it punishes. A big enough hit still fells you before you swing back.

34a115db-3153-aff4-9a46-97700634f1fb.description: A gilded feint that sends the foe stumbling before the real blow lands — the workhorse of a champion's kit.

3e76f2fa-072c-5654-dac9-c9b0e4e1643a.description: An overloaded sugar rush poured over the WHOLE team: every living ally, you included, is pulled back from the brink. Pick no target — it reaches them all. It will not raise the fallen, and you only have the sugar for it TWICE per duel.

41416afb-0d72-f9b4-ba30-f089b713500f.description: Hardens into a glassy candy shell. It soaks damage before your health does and KEEPS whatever is left over for later rounds. Careful: it goes up when YOUR turn comes, not the moment the round starts — a faster foe strikes before it exists. A shield is an investment, not a reaction. Deals no damage, and you need a round to set another.

431900a2-54d4-df34-d9f7-6a4608fecfaa.description: Goes soft and gummy and ABSORBS. It soaks damage before your health does and keeps whatever survives into later rounds. Careful: it goes up when YOUR turn comes, not the moment the round starts — a faster foe strikes before it exists. A shield is an investment, not a reaction. Deals no damage, and you need a round to set another.

4dbb297f-6a80-ca44-a94f-3ea9ed3311e8.description: A tarnished-silver crouch: a light kick, and for the rest of the round you shrug off most of whatever lands on you. Ready again the very next round. It is the SAME block a Common throws — an Epic's edge is that it also holds a guard, a counter and a team-wide ward, not that its wall is thicker.

55cafe03-4a58-0f44-6a27-f9fc6fce881a.description: Stamps the ground so hard the candy floor buckles and throws a wall up around the WHOLE team: every living ally, you included, is warded. Pick no target — it reaches them all. Each ward soaks damage before health does and keeps whatever survives for later rounds. It gives YOU no more than an ordinary shield would; what it buys is that your team gets one too. Deals no damage, and it is a long wait for the next one.

55e05ece-a3f6-a924-3833-2222a0eee6a3.description: Flings TWO razor-edged lime candies in a single breath. They land as two separate strikes — each clashes on its own, each can fell the foe, and each is punished on its own by a counter. If the first one kills, the second is never thrown.

61d8b3ec-9a01-0e64-fb45-27d2b858e332.description: Lobs a jawbreaker packed with popping powder into the middle of the enemy team — it detonates on EVERY living foe at once, though the blast is thinner than a focused blow. Pick no target: it catches them all. It goes straight through a Guard, but every foe holding a counter still hits back.

628ebc0b-bb19-ee64-68b8-2f66f4b92720.description: A blur of paper-thin candy shards — many quick nicks rather than one big cut. The heaviest of the quick strikes, and always ready.

73eb61fc-70a9-25a4-ba01-0ecaa0d547c5.description: Raises a crystallized sugar shield. It soaks damage before your health does and carries whatever survives into later rounds. Careful: it goes up when YOUR turn comes, not the moment the round starts — a faster foe strikes before it exists. A shield is an investment, not a reaction. Deals no damage, and you need a round to set another. Every shield in the game grants exactly this much: the rarest treat's is no thicker.

86b323c2-b2fd-2494-ab5e-bc3514bc92d8.description: A basic candy-coated haymaker — dependable weight behind every swing, and the hardest blow a newcomer can throw. Slow, but never on cooldown.

86bd4f81-e96b-9864-78b5-5fc51fbff6a4.description: A frosty strike thrown from range that chills on impact — reach and weight together, at the cost of a round to wind up again.

8e4753b5-c739-aaf4-ab79-4d333a69cae6.description: A bouncing gilded riposte: strike, then hurl a good part of any blow that lands on you this round back at its owner. It soaks NOTHING — it punishes, and it punishes every attacker who touches you. It is the SAME counter a Rare throws; an Epic simply also owns a block, a guard and a team ward.

92f7b745-c1ff-3cd4-f9cf-0fe0dafb5536.description: Rains gilded chocolate chips down over the WHOLE enemy team — every living foe is caught in the downpour, each taking less than a focused blow would deal. Pick no target: it catches them all. It goes straight through a Guard, but every foe holding a counter still hits back.

9f0df0ed-13a6-84c4-781d-9dbf9741c66e.description: A crunching chocolate-bar clothesline — solid, no-frills power, and a round to reset.

9fd0b9ef-2e7d-d344-8926-69f1fab99f65.description: A fistful of popping candy detonated point-blank — quicker off the mark than a proper haymaker, lighter for it, and always ready.

a9d69fc0-a1b8-ae24-cbd7-249760200211.description: A bubblegum bubble lobbed from afar that pops right on target — quick, light and always ready.

aa7d8565-99e1-84f4-49d2-8925c2602a7b.description: Winds up and hurls a rock-hard jawbreaker — brutal, and quicker off the mark than it has any right to be. You will need two rounds before the next one.

ad6d5d28-08b4-4304-6b49-11f8f5f9ff1c.description: Pours a caffeinated rush of sugar into an ALLY — or into yourself — hauling them back on their feet. Pick a friend, not a foe. It cannot raise a fallen treat: a KO is forever, and healing the dead does nothing at all. Deals no damage.

b1bd32de-88b2-1204-7b2e-0680677d78c4.description: Slides a caramel puddle underfoot and closes the gap in a flash — nothing in the arena is quicker.

b889835e-1cd6-d3d4-f9b4-dd1989f49dac.description: A cheeky low blow to the jellybean midsection — as rude as it is effective.

b96f1ff8-66d5-f244-79fd-260735d4c3d7.description: An unsettling gut-churner of a hit that leaves the target queasy and off their game — the heaviest ordinary blow a champion throws.

bbf71f26-78f5-5014-5b4b-a1c3866cb75b.description: A crackling metallic coating whose charge ARCS to every living foe at once — thinner than a focused blow, but nobody escapes it. Pick no target: the chain finds them all. It goes straight through a Guard, but every foe holding a counter still hits back.

bd27fe1c-ce9a-f524-c887-61f9e4d5088b.description: A low sweeping kick that scatters loose sugar and knocks the foe off balance. Nothing fancy — and nothing to wait for.

c28e13b4-cb1f-6ea4-680f-91010a400e10.description: A snappy jab aimed straight at the sweet spot — quick, precise, and always ready.

c6770789-4a1f-7f24-f921-c4dc66c2697a.description: Sprays a shimmering hail of golden shot across the whole arena — EVERY living foe is peppered, though no one of them as hard as a focused blow. Pick no target: the volley covers them all. It goes straight through a Guard, but every foe holding a counter still hits back.

e16531a7-33fd-a3e4-69fd-ff10eba65cb7.description: A licorice haymaker thrown with everything behind it — the heaviest swing that isn't an ultimate. Slow, and it leaves an opening.

e201f964-8949-9d74-fbee-4ee8786284ca.description: A fiery cinnamon burst that scatters over EVERY living foe — a thin splash, but a fast one, and it reaches the whole team. Pick no target. It goes straight through a Guard, but every foe holding a counter still hits back.

e55a3d69-9fb4-5194-4812-c2f85e887aca.description: A knuckle-duster forged from hardened silver taffy, swung with everything you have — the single hardest blow in the game. You only have the strength to throw it TWICE per duel, and you will wait for it in between.

fa6144f9-ad49-8124-386f-351a7f1ab546.description: Drifts in soft as butter cream, then lands a hit the foe never saw coming.
